<!--
Thanks for submitting benchmark results via PR. Please:
1. Place your CSV in `Runtimes/submissions/` (do NOT edit `ByscayneMode_Benchmarks.xlsx` directly — the maintainer integrates it).
2. Fill out the metadata table below.
3. Confirm the methodology checklist.

Filename convention (produced automatically by the automated scripts):
  {ComputerName}_{executable}_{YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSS}_benchmark_results.csv
  e.g. WorkM5_usgt_180_arm_2026-04-07_210000_benchmark_results.csv
-->

## Submitted CSV

- **File:** `Runtimes/submissions/<filename>.csv`
- **Sweep range:** <e.g. 1–16 agents / single batch of 8 / range 10–16>

## Machine metadata

| Field             | Value |
|-------------------|-------|
| Computer name     | <e.g. WorkM5>                            |
| CPU model         | <e.g. Apple M5>                          |
| CPU cores / speed | <e.g. 10c — 4.61 GHz>                    |
| RAM               | <e.g. 32 GB unified>                     |
| OS                | <e.g. macOS 15.3>                        |
| CPU architecture  | <ARM / x86>                              |
| Executable used   | <e.g. usgt_180_arm — ARM64 gfortran>     |
| Manufacturer      | <e.g. Apple>                             |
| Form factor       | <e.g. MacBook Pro Laptop / Mac Mini>     |
| Cooling           | <Stock / Aftermarket air / AIO / etc.>   |

## Methodology

- [ ] Used the automated script (`run_benchmark_suite.sh` / `.ps1`) — sequential, no pause between batches
- [ ] Used the manual workflow (`copy_files` → `open_and_run` → `clean_up`, individually triggered)

If this submission is part of a paired x86-vs-ARM comparison on the same machine, link the companion run here:
- Companion run: <PR or Issue link, or "first half of pair — companion to follow">

## Pre-submission checklist

- [ ] CSV was generated by the official `retrieve_runtimes.py` (no manual edits)
- [ ] Machine had no other significant compute load during the run
- [ ] CSV filename follows the auto-generated convention
- [ ] Did NOT edit `Runtimes/ByscayneMode_Benchmarks.xlsx` directly
- [ ] Did NOT modify any file under `benchmark_model/`

> **Note on CSV visibility:** `Runtimes/submissions/*.csv` files are gitignored on purpose — the maintainer integrates them manually into the master spreadsheet. Your CSV will not appear in the PR diff, which is expected. What matters is that it is physically present in the folder when the PR is opened; mention the filename in the description above so the maintainer can confirm receipt.

## Notes
